violations.text: At the time of inspection, several cheese products were previously placed under embargo. Owner could not provide necessary documentation that the cheese products come from an approved source. PIC voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

At the time of inspection, several cheese products were observed for sale that originated from Honduras, per interview with owner. PIC could not provide necessary information of approved source for the cheese products. All cheese products were placed under embargo at the time of inspection. Spanish Approved Food Source Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2024

At the time of inspection, several cheese products were observed for sale without proper labeling. Per interview with PIC, cheese comes from Honduras. PIC could not provide necessary information of approved source of the cheese products. All cheese products were placed under Embargo at the time of inspection. Spanish Approved Food Source New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2024

Various cheese products, desserts and meat products were lacking labels. Food must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2024
